change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Political cartoons concerning France
did.abstract: French Revolution period caricatures mainly concerning the three estates of France, the nationalization of church property, privileges of the clergy and nobility, despotism, the constitution of 1791, and the political process.
did.langmaterial: Collection material in French and German

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Related materials are held at Cornell in the Lafayette Collection; the Graphics, Pamphlets, Newspapers, and Books components of the French Revolution Collection; and the general Rare Books collection. All are located in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.

Former call numbers: Archives DC140.9 .F87 Box 1-5+++ (under title French Revolution political caricatures); Archives DC140.9 .F87 Box 7-12+++ (under title Nineteenth-century political caricatures concerning France). Items housed in Archives DC140.9 .F87 Box 7-12+++ included an album of anti-Napoleon caricatures with previous call number Rare DC203.4C27++.

emph.ref: Series I. Attacks against the Old Rgime and Legal Privileges (before July 1789)
Series II. Storming of the Bastille (14 July 1789)
Series III. Abolition of Aristocracy and Nationalization of Church Property (August-November 1789)
Series IV. Celebration of National Unity, Fte de la Federation (14 July 1790), Constitutional Monarchy
Series V. Satire against Royalists, Emigrs, and the Conservative Church
Series VI. Outside Perception of the French Revolution
Series VII. Revolutionary War in Europe (1791-1798)
Series VIII. Comité de Salut Public and "Reign of Terror"
Series IX. Fall of Robespierre, Thermidorian Reaction
Series X. Reign of Napoleon I. Official Propaganda Celebrating His Victories as well as the New Dynasty
Series X. Hostile Caricatures and French-Bashing (1813-1815)
Series XI. The First Bourbon Restauration (April 1814-March 1815) and the Cent-Jours (March-July 1815)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Political Cartoons Concerning France, 1789-1914, #8552. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: French Revolution period caricatures mainly concerning the three estates of France, the nationalization of church property, privileges of the clergy and nobility, despotism, the constitution of 1791, and the political process. Included are Adieu Bastille, Le Dentiste National, and La Nation Française Assistée de M. de la Fayette. Includes one German caricature by P. Scherz, engraved by Brunee. Napoleonic period caricatures consist mainly of hand-colored engravings and etchings, many of them French or German caricatures of Napoleon which satirize his military setbacks or note the carnage caused by his military campaigns. These include Triumph des Jahres 1813; Ach! Papa, Welche schöne Seifenblasen hast du gemacht; Georges édenté; Der Rheinische Courier; and others.All French Revolution period prints are anonymous except for one item by Benjamin, lithographed by Becquet, and one engraving drawn by Geissler. Artists for later prints include J.J. Grandville, Jean-Baptiste Isabey, and Charles Philipon. Most of the prints were issued by anonymous publishers. Named publishers: Paris: Basset, Martinet, Genty, Aubert, and Crépy.

Talleyrand-Périgord, Charles Maurice de, prince de Bénévent, 1754-1838 -- : Caricatures and cartoons.
Wellington, Arthur Wellesley, Duke of, 1769-1852 -- : Caricatures and cartoons.
controlaccess.corpname: Congress of Vienna (Date of meeting or treaty signing: (1814-1815).)
controlaccess.persname: Marie Antoinette, Queen, consort of Louis XVI, King of France, 1755-1793

did.unittitle: Attacks against the Old Regime and Legal Privileges (before July 1789)
"A Faut esperer queu se jeu…"
did.physdesc: Print shows the nobility and the clergy riding on the back of a man representing the third estate or working class; the man is bent under their weight and supports, while around his feet birds and rabbits (which he cannot hunt) feast on the produce of his labor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Described as follows in the Catalogue of the Library of A.D. White (Cornell, 1894): "Colored engraving showing a bishop and a nobleman astride of an old peasant, who moves slowly, supported by his mattock, while pigeons and hares devour his grain and vegetables about his feet... For a sequel of this caricature, see J'savois ben, etc." (p. 293)

did.unittitle: "La France Figurée sous un Globe est soutenu du Peuple. La Noblesse et le Clergé"
Storming of the Bastille (14 July 1789)
"Réveil du Tiers-Etat: "Ma feinte, il doit tems que je me réveillisse [sic]…"
"Adieu Bastille"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available on Shared Shelf Commons: RMC2007_0369

did.unittitle: Fan depicting the storming of the Bastille
Abolition of Aristocracy and Nationalization of Church Property (August-November 1789)
"J'savois ben qu'j'aurions not' tour"
"Ha je seront bien content quand j'aurons tous ces papiers la"
"Ils les avaients [sic] trop longs"
"Les Trois ordres… sous le niveau"
"L'Abb d'autre fois. L'Abb d'aujourd'hui"
"Grand mal de cœur de Monseigneur : Courage Mrg vous allé́ vous purgée [sic] de chose bien utile pour votre salut"
"Le Dentiste National"
"Trois têtes sous le même bonnet"
"Ma finte, Monsieur, je crois que vot' habit d'officier m'ira ben !"
"Un seul fait les trois"
"Les sacrifices forces"
Celebration of National Unity, Fete de la Federation (14 July 1790), Constitutional Monarchy
"Bon nous voilà d'accord"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available on Shared Shelf Common: RMC2009_0125

did.unittitle: "Par moi vous tes tous frres"
"Tôt tôt tôt battez chaud tôt tôt tôt bon courrage il faut avoir cœur à l'ouvrage"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available on Shared Shelf Common; RMC2007_0367

did.unittitle: No Title, showing the three estates as part of the same carriage
"Magicienne Consultée sur la Révolution de 1789 : orgueil dompté, avarice punie, mérite reconnû [sic], union des quatre parties du monde"
"La Nation française assistée de Mr de la Fayette terrasse le despotisme et les abus du règne féodal…"
"Ha, je seront bien content quand j'aurons tous ces papiers-là : Le trésor tiré des ténèbres"
Satire against Royalists, Emigrés, and the Conservative Church
"Le Pied de Nez ou l'Aristocratie écrasée"

Un Monstre a trois têtes désignant les trois Etats de l'aristocratie s'occupe à dévorer le reste du cadavre du peuple qu'il à englouti impitoyablement dans ses entrailles carnivores : il est précédé du fanatisme aux queus [sic] de dragon et qui est vêtu d'un froc monacal, il porte à califourchon sur son dos l'hypocrisie pressante, un serpent qui distille son poison aristocratique
[High Clergy, Parliaments, and Nobility, in the Persons of Maury, d'Eprémesnil and Mirabeau-Tonneau] « On ne peut trop les punir, ils nous ont fait assez souffrir : Labor omnia vincit improbus »
"Monsieur Veto : quel est donc ce Mr Veto, qui seul fait plus de bruit que n'en fit Figaro sans doute il n'est pas né dans le canton de Berne. Veut du peuple faire un zéro, etc"

"Curé de St ....... accompagné de deux diables descend dans l'empire des démons pour demander a Belzebuth leurs princes des secours pour tacher s'il est possible d'empêcher l'exécution de la Constitution civile ecclésiastique. Ou va-t'en au diable"
"Le Tiers-Etat mariant les religieux avec les religieuses: en faisant une si bonne action, nous garantissons des cornes"
"Saint Pierre Reniant le Pape Pie VI"

"M. le marquis de Vieille Roche protestant contre les Decrets de l'Assemblée Nationale ; M. le vieux Marechal Aristocrate effraié de la bourasque parisienne ; L'Officier Aristocrate mettant les pouces devant les patriotes français ; M. le Chevalier, ancien Courtisan sans titres ni pension"
"Retour de Deux Emigrans"
Outside Perception of the French Revolution
"D Frankreichs Lillie!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains optical illusion images of 14 heads of state and political revolutionaries including Robespierre, Hebert, and Orleans

did.unittitle: "Grand convoi funebre de leurs majestés les Jacobins en leur vivant, nos seigneurs et maitres décédé en leurs palais de la rue St. Honoré"

origination.label: Creator
origination.persname: Massard, J. B. L. ((Jean Baptiste Louis)), 1772-1810

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows Jacobins masquerading as animals in a carnival procession, led by Brissot de Warville and Condorcet, who are riding asses (facing backwards). Jean-Paul Marat is at the rear.

controlaccess.persname: Condorcet, Jean-Antoine-Nicolas de Caritat, marquis de, 1743-1794

did.unittitle: Revolutionary War in Europe (1791-1798)

"Subsistances portées par le duc Dyork à Toulon : on voit ce qu'il y avait dans le sac ; le grain seul reste sur le terrein de la liberté́, qu'il abandonne, ayant le nez cassé et le sceptre ses espérances étant rompu"
"Départ de l'Anglais du continent : chargé de son moulin sans grain, portant sa gentille meunière, ce triste Anglais s'en va bon train, qu'and Jacquot lui souffle au derrière"
"Die grosse und merkwurdige Regierungsrevolution der Bataver Republick… am 12 Juni 1798"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: As a general in the French revolutionary army of General Charles Pichegru and commander of the Batavian Legion. Daendels helped Dutch politician Pieter Vreede to power in a coup d'tat on 25 January 1798. The reign of Vreede did not bring the expected results, however, and Daendels supported another coup d'tat against Vreede on 14 June 1798]

did.unittitle: An Emblematical View of the Constitutions of England and France
Comité de Salut Public and "Reign of Terror"
"Vive la LibertVive la Libert"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Described as follows in the Catalogue of the Historical Library of A.D. White (1894): "Colored engraving representing a prisoner in a dimly lighted cell, standing on a bunch of straw, chained by waist and neck to the wall, and uttering the above exclamation" (p. 296)

did.unittitle: "Guillotine"
Fall of Robespierre, Thermidorian Reaction
"Signalement Des Chouans et Autres Contrevolutionaires [sic]: Officiers gnraux de l'arme thermidorienne sortant du Caff de Chartres allant en expditions arms de btons"
"Ėmigrand [sic] revenant a Paris"
"Le Petit Coblentz"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available on Shared Shelf Commons: RMC2007_0368

did.unittitle: Reign of Napoleon I. Official Propaganda Celebrating His Victories as well as the New Dynasty
Official Propaganda Celebrating His Victories as well as the New Dynasty
Entrevue de Napolon le Grand, 1.er Empereur des Franais et Roi d'Italie, avec Franois II. Empereur d'Allemagne [after Austerlitz, 1805]
Combat de Saalfeld et Mort du Prince Louis de Prusse
"Georges [King George III. of England], dent. Quand il n'aura pas plus de dents il ne mordra plus!"

"Trait de prudence du Lord Morpeth: Envoy d'Angleterre auprs du Cabinet Prussien il ne se trouvait qu'a 6 lieues du champ de bataille pendant la journe d'Jena il entend le canon ... court ... offre 60 guines pour un cheval l'obtient et se sauve"
"Famille Impériale et Royale de Napoléon le Grand premier du nom, Empereur des Français, Roi d'Italie, et Protecteur de la Confédération du Rhin"
"Promenade du Petit Roi de Rome au bord de l'eau"
Hostile Caricatures and French-Bashing (1813-1815)
"Triumph des Jahres 1813"
"Die neue Europaeische Barbierstube"
"A la Cosaka"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: "Der neue Universalmonarch"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available on Shared Shelf Commons: RMC2008_1148

did.unittitle: "Der grosse Totengraber"
"Ach! Papa, welche schone Seifenblasen hast du gemacht!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Available on Shared Shelf Commons: RM2008-1147

did.unittitle: "Saute pour le roi"
"Je Fume en Pleurant mes Peches"
The First Bourbon Restoration (April 1814-March 1815) and the Cent-Jours (March-July 1815)
"La Colre du ciel est enfin apaise, nos Rois nous sont rendus"
"Une visite de l'Ile d'Elbe, ou l'embarras de la toilette" [Louis XVIII and his brother, future Charles X]
"M. de la Rodomontade Apprenant le Debarquement de l'Empereur"
"Depart de Paris de Mr. de la Jobardire: Il est revenu! Filons!"
"Le Zélé Défenseur"
"La Girouette"
"C'est aujourd'hui la St. Lambert qui quitte sa place la perd"
"Retour de l'Ile d'Elbe, Il Ramène la Liberté!"
Second Restoration (1815-1830)
"La Balance Politique" [Congress of Vienna, 1815]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plate from "Le Nain Jaune"

did.unittitle: "Serment des Nouveaux Horace : Nous le Soutiendrons!" [Louis XVIII]
"Ah quelle crise ! Soutenez-moi car je chancelle" [Louis XVIII, Blacas, and Montesquiou]
"A ton tour mon vieux!" [Charles X and Hussein Dey, Ottoman ruler of Algiers, 1830]
"Nouveau Journal des Modes : Coiffure a la Rambouillet inventée et exécutée par la population parisienne [pour Charles X]"
"Armes du Grand Poulot" [Imaginary Coat of Arms of King Louis-Philippe I.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plate from "La caricature", nr. 63. Mentioned in the diary of Count Apponyi, the Austrian ambassador in Paris, 19 January 1832: "On a saisi hier une caricature : Les armes du grand Poulot; c'est un cusson bandes, une blanche entre deux vertes, deux btons tricolores avec un paon, queue dploye ; pour support un cheval de carton, des capucins de cartes, arms de fusils, des canards de carte, enfin des joujoux d'enfants ; pour couronne, un bonnet de papier avec un bouquet de lierre et pour devise : Bienheureux les pauvres d'esprit parce que le royaume est eux."

did.unittitle: "Voici le pistolet qui a si longtemps amus le public, Voici la poire poudre, la poupe du tir et les drles de balles dont on s'est servi [after an attempt to assassinate King Louis-Philippe on November 19, 1832]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plate from "La Caricature", nr. 130

did.unittitle: "Les Favoris de la Poire" [Louis-Philippe as a pear with two of his ministers, Antoine d'Argout and Felix Barthe]

did.unittitle: "N'oubliez pas le postillon, bourgeois : c'est juste, voici une poignée de main" [King Louis-Philippe as a penny-pinching bourgeois]

did.unittitle: "Die neue Constitution bringt Schaden [an der] Kirch und Nation / La nouvelle Constitution: nuit a l'eglise et a la nation"

did.unittitle: "Dies ist mein lieber John am den ich wohligfallen habe"
"Avance"
"An emblematical view of the constitutions of England and France exhibiting at the same time..."
Der Rheinische Courier three prints
German caricature by P. Scherz, drawn by Brunee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purchased in Vienna in 1889 by Andrew Dickson White.
